name: Product Cards
description: How individual product cards should present key information to support confident browsing and comparison decisions

heuristics.principle: Each product card should display the minimum information needed for a purchase-relevant decision: image, name, and price
checkpoints: Product image is clearly visible and appropriately sized
Product name or title is readable without truncation of key details
Price is prominently displayed on every card
Sale or discounted prices show both original and reduced price

heuristics.principle: Product images should be high quality and consistently sized to allow visual comparison across the grid
checkpoints: Images are sharp and not pixelated or stretched
All product images use a consistent aspect ratio
Product is clearly visible against the background
Hover or secondary image shows the product from another angle where available

heuristics.principle: Available colour or variant swatches should be shown on the card to reduce unnecessary PDP visits
checkpoints: Colour swatches or variant indicators are visible on the card
Hovering on a swatch updates the product image
Number of additional variants is indicated if space is limited
Swatch interaction does not navigate away from the listing page

heuristics.principle: Review ratings and availability status should be visible on cards to aid decision-making before clicking through
checkpoints: Star rating or review count is displayed where reviews exist
Out of stock products are clearly marked
Bestseller or trending badges provide social proof
Low stock indicators create appropriate urgency without being misleading

heuristics.principle: Quick-add or quick-view functionality should reduce friction for users who know what they want
checkpoints: Quick add to cart is available for simple products without variants
Quick view modal shows key details without full PDP navigation
Quick actions do not obscure essential card information
Quick add confirms success with clear feedback
